---
title: "ST2195 Coursework Part 2"
author: "Divya Nistala"
date: "2025-03-29"
output: html_document
---
## 2
I have chosen to use the 5 consecutive years, from 2002-2006, to help me with my analysis. I will also be using any additional information provided by the Harvard dataset to further my research.

## Preparation of Analysis
## Required packages
library(DBI)
library(RSQLite)
library(knitr)
library(dplyr) 
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales) 
library(reshape2)
library(lubridate)
library(gplots) 
library(ggpubr)
library(caret) 
library(ROCit)
library(AUC)
library(nnet)
library(ROSE)
library(stats) 
library(gridExtra)
library(readr)
library(data.table)
library(broom)

# Set Working Directory to Harvard Dataverse Files
setwd("C:/Users/divya/Downloads/Uni/ST2195/ST2195/Coursework/dataverse_files")
getwd()

## Planes dataset

# Create connection the database named "airline2_r.db"
conn <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), "airline2_r.db")
dbConnect(conn)

# Create data tables in database 
airports <- read.csv("airports.csv", header = TRUE)
carriers <- read.csv("carriers.csv", header = TRUE)
planes <- read.csv("plane-data.csv", header = TRUE)

dbWriteTable(conn, "airports", airports, overwrite = TRUE )
dbWriteTable(conn, "carriers", carriers, overwrite = TRUE )
dbWriteTable(conn, "planes", planes, overwrite = TRUE )


## Load in chosen 5 years of consecutive data (2002-2006)

for(i in c(2002:2006)) {
  filename <- paste0(i, ".csv.bz2")
  print(paste("Processing:", filename))
  
  
  ontime <- read.csv(filename, header = TRUE)
  if(i == 2002) {
    dbWriteTable(conn, "ontime", ontime, overwrite = TRUE)
  } else {
    dbWriteTable(conn, "ontime", ontime, append = TRUE)
  }
}

## Part 2(a)

# Add Indexes to improve performance 
dbExecute(conn, "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_year ON ontime (Year);")
dbExecute(conn, "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dayofweek ON ontime (DayOfWeek);")
dbExecute(conn, "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_deptime ON ontime (DepTime);")

# Filter 5 years of data into Day of the Week 
avg_delay_by_day <- tbl(conn, "ontime") %>%
  filter(Year >= 2002 & Year <= 2006) %>%
  group_by(Year, DayOfWeek) %>%
  summarise(AvgDelay = mean(ArrDelay, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  collect()  # Summarized data brought into memory

# Data Preparation for Time of Day Plot
Delays <- tbl(conn, "ontime") %>%
  filter(ArrDelay > 0 & Year >= 2002 & Year <= 2006) %>%
  mutate(
    TimeofDay = case_when(
      CRSDepTime %in% 500:1159  ~ "Morning",
      CRSDepTime %in% 1200:1659 ~ "Afternoon",
      CRSDepTime %in% 1700:2059 ~ "Evening",
      TRUE ~ "Night"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(Year, DayOfWeek, TimeofDay) %>%
  summarise(AvgDelay = mean(ArrDelay, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  collect() %>%  
  mutate(
    Day = factor(DayOfWeek, labels = c("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"))
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

# Plot Average Delay timings against Day of the Week for all the 5 years  
plot_day <- ggplot(avg_delay_by_day, aes(x = factor(DayOfWeek, labels = c("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday")), y = AvgDelay, fill = factor(Year))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Average Delay by Day of the Week (2002-2006)", 
       y = "Average Delay (minutes)", 
       x = "Day of the Week") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1))

# Plot Average Delay timings against Time of Day for all the 5 years 
plot_time_of_day <- ggplot(Delays, aes(x = TimeofDay, y = AvgDelay, fill = TimeofDay)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1), 
        legend.position = "none") +
  xlab("Time of Day") +
  ylab("Average Arrival Delay (in Minutes)") + 
  ggtitle("Average Arrival Delay by Time of Day (2002-2006)")

# Combine and display the plots together
plot_day + plot_time_of_day

## Part 2(b)

# Same list of years to analyse
years <- 2002:2006

# Load and clean the plane data 
planes <- read_csv("plane-data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  rename_all(tolower) %>%
  mutate(
    issue_date = as.Date(issue_date, format = "%m/%d/%Y"),
    issue_year = as.numeric(format(issue_date, "%Y"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(issue_year))  # Only keep rows with valid manufacture year

# Create plots to store correlation outputs
plot_list_2b <- list()
correlation_summary <- list()

# Repeat for each of the 5 years 
for (year in years) {
  message("Processing year: ", year)
  filename <- paste0(year, ".csv.bz2")
  
  # Load flight data and preprocess
  ontime <- read_csv(filename, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    select(TailNum, ArrDelay, Year) %>%
    rename(
      tailnum = TailNum,
      arr_delay = ArrDelay,
      flight_year = Year
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(tailnum), !is.na(arr_delay), arr_delay >= 0)
  
# Combine with our plane data
  merged <- inner_join(ontime, planes, by = "tailnum") %>%
    mutate(
      plane_age = flight_year - issue_year
    ) %>%
    filter(plane_age >= 0)  # Remove invalid ages

# To identify null years to exclude from analysis
  if (nrow(merged) == 0) next
  
# Summarize average delay timings by plane age
  grouped <- merged %>%
    group_by(plane_age) %>%
    summarise(
      average_delay = mean(arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    drop_na()

# To again identify null years to exclude from analysis
  if (nrow(grouped) < 2) next

# Generate plots, red scatter plots with black regression line 
  plot <- ggscatter(
    grouped, x = "plane_age", y = "average_delay",
    color = "red",                 
    add = "reg.line", add.params = list(color = "black"), 
    xlab = "Age of Aircraft (Years)",
    ylab = "Average Arrival Delay (Minutes)",
    title = paste("Year", year)
  )

# Save the generated plots 
  plot_list_2b[[as.character(year)]] <- plot

# Now, we conduct the Correlation test
  corr_test <- cor.test(grouped$plane_age, grouped$average_delay, method = "pearson")
  r_value <- corr_test$estimate
  r_classification <- case_when(
    r_value > 0.87 ~ "strong positive",
    r_value > 0.5  ~ "moderate positive",
    r_value > 0    ~ "weak positive",
    r_value > -0.5 ~ "weak negative",
    r_value > -0.87 ~ "moderate negative",
    TRUE ~ "strong negative"
  )

# Save Correlation summary
  correlation_summary[[as.character(year)]] <- list(
    r = r_value,
    classification = r_classification,
    message = paste(
      "Year", year, ": A correlation coefficient of",
      round(r_value, 2), "indicates a", r_classification,
      "correlation between aircraft age and arrival delay."
    )
  )
}

# Print correlation messages to explain strength of relationship
for (res in correlation_summary) {
  print(res$message)
}

# Combine plots together 
if (length(plot_list_2b) > 0) {
  wrap_plots(plot_list_2b) + plot_layout(ncol = 2)
} else {
  print("No plots to display.")
}

## 2(c)

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

years <- 2002:2006
top_n <- 10  # Number of top coefficients to show per year
plot_list <- list()  # Store plots

# Load and clean the airport data
airports <- read_csv("airports.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  rename(latitude = lat, longitude = long) %>%
  select(iata, latitude, longitude) %>%
  distinct()

airports_origin <- airports %>%
  rename(Origin = iata, origin_lat = latitude, origin_lon = longitude)

airports_dest <- airports %>%
  rename(Dest = iata, dest_lat = latitude, dest_lon = longitude)

for (year in years) {
  message("Processing year: ", year)
  filename <- paste0(year, ".csv.bz2")
  
# Load necessary predictor variables from each of the 5 selected years
  ontime <- read_csv(filename, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    select(Diverted, Month, DayOfWeek, CRSDepTime, CRSArrTime,
           Distance, Origin, Dest, UniqueCarrier) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Diverted)) %>%
    mutate(
      diverted_flag = ifelse(Diverted == 1, 1, 0),
      dep_hour = floor(CRSDepTime / 100),
      arr_hour = floor(CRSArrTime / 100)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(dep_hour), !is.na(arr_hour), Distance > 0)

# Combine filtered data with airport coordinates data
  ontime <- ontime %>%
    inner_join(airports_origin, by = "Origin") %>%
    inner_join(airports_dest, by = "Dest") %>%
    mutate(
      UniqueCarrier = factor(UniqueCarrier),
      DayOfWeek = factor(DayOfWeek)
    )

# Combine the datasets to be plotted 
  model <- glm(
    diverted_flag ~ Month + DayOfWeek + dep_hour + arr_hour + Distance +
      origin_lat + origin_lon + dest_lat + dest_lon + UniqueCarrier,
    data = ontime,
    family = binomial()
  )

# We filter out the most popular predictors
  tidy_coef <- tidy(model) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(abs_estimate = abs(estimate)) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs_estimate)) %>%
    slice(1:top_n)

# Plot the coefficient ouputs as bar plots 
  p <- ggplot(tidy_coef, aes(x = reorder(term, estimate), y = estimate)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "brown", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = paste("Coefficients of Logistic Regression Model - Year", year),
         x = "Variables", y = "Coefficient") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10)

  plot_list[[as.character(year)]] <- p

# Just a clean up to save memory
  rm(ontime, model, tidy_coef)
  gc()
}

# Combine all the 5 year plots 
wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 2)
